name: Cambrils to Salou - circular route
region_intro: Touring cycling around Cambrils offers a diverse landscape, transitioning from flat coastal plains to rugged inland mountains. The region is characterized by extensive seafront promenades, fertile agricultural areas with orchards and olive groves, and challenging mountain ranges like the Prades Mountains and Sierra de LLaberia. This varied terrain provides a wide range of routes suitable for different cycling preferences and abilities. Best touring cycling routes around Cambrils The most popular touring cycling route is Cambrils to Salou - circular route, a 22.2 miles (35.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 9 minutes to complete. This route connects the coastal towns of Cambrils and Salou, offering continuous sea views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cambrils Marina – Cambrils Seafront Promenade loop from Mas d'en Bosch, a moderate 31.1 miles (50.1 km) path. This route explores the coastal stretch, featuring the marina and seafront promenade. Local touring cyclists also love the Coll de la Teixeta – Mont-roig del Camp Bike Path loop from Cambrils, a 37.6 miles (60.5 km) trail leading through the Coll de la Teixeta mountain pass, often completed in about 4 hours 3 minutes. Touring cycling around Cambrils is defined by flat coastal paths, demanding mountain passes, and routes through vineyards and olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from relaxed rides along the coast to challenging climbs inland. The routes in Cambrils are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 5000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Cambrils's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Cambrils?
A: Cambrils offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 130 options available. These routes cater to different skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when touring cycling around Cambrils?
A: The terrain around Cambrils is incredibly diverse. You can enjoy flat coastal routes along the seafront promenade, explore fertile agricultural areas with orchards and olive groves, or challenge yourself on demanding climbs through mountain ranges like the Prades Mountains, Montsant Natural Park, and Sierra de LLaberia. The region also features renowned wine areas like Priorat with pleasant, winding roads.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Cambrils?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Cambrils is generally outside of the hot summer months. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, making it ideal for exploring both coastal paths and inland mountain routes. These seasons provide comfortable conditions for longer rides, especially towards areas like the Ebro Delta.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or families in Cambrils?
A: Yes, Cambrils is very family-friendly and offers many routes suitable for beginners. The town has 24 km of flat bike lanes, and the 9km seafront promenade connecting Cambrils to Salou is perfect for relaxed cycling with continuous sea views. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33889935" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mermaid Statue – View of the Coves loop from Pal&lt;/a&gt;, which is rated as easy.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Cambrils provides access to routes with significant elevation gains and multiple mountain passes. Areas like Priorat, the Prades Mountains, and Montsant Natural Park offer demanding rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31445495" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de la Teixeta – Mont-roig del Camp Bike Path loop from Cambrils&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route that takes you through an emblematic mountain pass with spectacular views.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes in Cambrils?
A: Many routes offer stunning natural features and viewpoints. You can enjoy sweeping views back to the coast from the Prades Mountains, or panoramic vistas of the Baix Camp region from demanding climbs like La Mussara. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3064827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Mare de Déu de la Roca&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5133131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salou Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; are notable natural monuments and viewpoints you might encounter.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible by touring bike from Cambrils?
A: Yes, several routes lead to fascinating historical and cultural sites. You can cycle towards the UNESCO World Heritage site of Poblet Monastery, or visit the well-preserved Roman aqueduct, the Devil's Bridge (Pont del Diable), near Tarragona. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5137425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou Castle and Monastery&lt;/a&gt; is another significant historical landmark accessible via some routes.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes in Cambrils?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes in Cambrils are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e979223349/cambrils-to-salou-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambrils to Salou - circular route&lt;/a&gt;, offering continuous sea views. Another great loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10430058" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cambrils Seafront Promenade – Cambrils Marina loop from Cambrils&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Cambrils?
A: The touring cycling routes around Cambrils are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 5,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: Does Cambrils offer specific facilities for touring cyclists?
A: Yes, Cambrils is certified as a Sport Tourism Destination specializing in cycling. This means the town provides excellent facilities tailored to cyclists' needs, including flexible meal times, picnic preparation services, repair workshops, and secure bike storage at many accommodations.

Q: Can I find long-distance touring cycling options from Cambrils?
A: Cambrils serves as an excellent base for longer touring cycling adventures. You can extend your rides towards the Ebro Delta for extensive flat routes or embark on challenging multi-pass routes like the 'Ruta de los 6 puertos' which covers over 140 km with significant elevation gain, exploring the pre-coastal mountain ranges and vineyards.
tips: The cozy and authentic fishing village is known for the relaxed atmosphere that the life of fishermen brings with it. That quiet atmosphere is interrupted every working day around 17:00 by the fish auction in the harbor. Known in the far area for the high-quality fish that is sold here.

The lighthouse is located at the end of the Camino de Ronda and is one of the most visited places of interest in Salou. It was inaugurated in April 1858 and is located in a very beautiful natural environment where you can see native plants such as the asparagus, the white pine, the sea lily, the agave or the cerrillo, among others.

This church was built in the 16th century to replace the churches of Barenys and Vilafortuny destroyed by pirates. It is the historic seat of the parish church of Santa Maria de Vilafortuny (source: https://www.guiarepsol.com/es/fichas/monumento/ermita-del-mas-den-bosch-181935/)..
